title: Workhorse Earnings Show Major Cost Cuts While EV Orders Surge Q1 2025
text: Workhorse Q1 2025 earnings reveal major cost cuts while EV orders surge. Revenue increased to $4.3 million from $1.1 million in Q1 2024 on a comparable GAAP basis. The company delivered 21 vehicles in Q1 2025 compared to 5 vehicles in Q1 2025, representing significant progress in commercialization. The loss per share improved from -$3.73 in Q1 2025, but still missed analyst expectations by about 32.5%. For Q2 2026, the consensus sales estimate is $9.33 million. Full year 2026 revenue is forecast at $40.14 million with an estimated full-year revenue change of -4.14%, indicating expectations of a slight decline. Workhorse Group is forecast to grow earnings and revenue by 43.7% and 22.5% per annum respectively while EPS is expected to grow 82.7% per annum. However, WKHS is forecast to remain unprofitable over the next 3 years.
